name: libmath-random-isaac-xs-perl
title: libmath-random-isaac-xs-perl
summary: Perl implementation of the ISAAC PRNG (C/XS Accelerated)
description:
Perl implementation of the ISAAC PRNG (C/XS Accelerated)
Math::Random::ISAAC::XS implements the same interface as Math::Random::ISAAC
and can be used as a drop-in replacement. This is the recommended version of
the module, based on Bob Jenkins' reference implementation in C as described
in his paper at: <URL:http://burtleburtle.net/bob/rand/isaac.html>
.
If you install this package, then any code that uses Math::Random::ISAAC will
automagically benefit from this faster implementation. Performance increases
are somewhere on the order of 400-500%.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libmath-random-isaac-xs-perl because Perl implementation of the ISAAC PRNG (C/XS Accelerated).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libmath-random-isaac-xs-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libmath-random-isaac-xs-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libmath-random-isaac-xs-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
